title: The Human Costs of the Angel Attacks
display_title: The Human Costs of the Angel Attacks
wikitext:
[]: The Human Costs of the Angel Attacks             Quote Post by Replicator » Wed Oct 31, 2012 8:13 am In Tokyo-III, when the city is attacked by Angels, what would be extent of the damage done towards the citizens or property? Like in 2.22 when Sahaquiel is destroyed, torrents of blood gushes through Tokyo-III, or the time Leliel exploded. Sure the civilians might have been evacuated, but what about the 'fake story' NERV makes up to explain why they all have to go into the shelters every week or two? Do the civilians (early in the series, at least) have any real idea of who's attacking and wrecking the city? I've never really understood for example, when Ramiel is dismantled, it took 3 episodes to finish the job. Did Tokyo-III return to business as usual with a giant corpse in downtown? And what about when NGE Sahaquiel drops 'bombs' on Japan from space? Surely there'd have been massive damage, judging from the size of the craters?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Oct 31, 2012 12:49 pm The citizens are aware there are some beings called "Angels" that are attacking the city, that only the evangelions seem to be able to defeat them, and that something really bad would happen if they destroy Tokyo-3. It might be worth noting that it's likely almost all, if not all, of Tokyo-3 works for NERV or is associated with them in some way, so i think they likely know whatever their security clearance allows them to. And yes, Tokyo-3 most likely kept working while they were dismantling Ramiel, and after Sahaquiel dropped the bombs on it. A recurrent theme on the series is how Tokyo-3 is the "Fortress of humanity" Mankind's greatest city and one of the main things that the pilots "protect", and in a display of humanity's willpower, the city braves on throught all of the attacks and hardships that come it's way. At least until episode 24 or so, but by then the series moves to a different angle anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Oct 31, 2012 12:57 pm I feel like the citizens who actually stay in Tokyo-3 are suffering from some kind of "Angel Grove" syndrome. After the second or third Angel attack, what reason would they have for staying and risking their lives?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Oct 31, 2012 1:53 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I feel like the citizens who actually stay in Toky-3 are suffering from some kind of "Angel Grove" syndrome. After the second or third Angel attack, what reason would they have for staying and risking their lives?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Oct 31, 2012 1:58 pm Dream wrote: they are employed by them.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Oct 31, 2012 4:00 pm I wonder if there's some massive "Doing the good of humanity" propaganda involved to get people to stay. Make them think (not entirely untrue though it may be) that the work they do in maintaining the city and Nerv benefits all mankind and that they're the best of the best and all that jazz.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Oct 31, 2012 4:12 pm CJD wrote: "Doing the good of humanity"

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Oct 31, 2012 4:16 pm It's not propaganda if it's true. Stopping the Angels is integral to the survival of the human race. Besides, in an Earth that has recently endured Second Impact and WWIII, is it really all that bad living in a first-world city that is among - if not THE - most heavily-fortified and defended single city on the planet?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Oct 31, 2012 4:22 pm Der Kommissar wrote: It's not propaganda if it's true. Stopping the Angels is integral to the survival of the human race. Besides, in an Earth that has recently endured Second Impact and WWIII, is it really all that bad living in a first-world city that is among - if not THE - most heavily-fortified and defended single city on the planet?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Oct 31, 2012 4:33 pm Der Kommissar wrote: is it really all that bad living in a first-world city that is among - if not THE - most heavily-fortified and defended single city on the planet?

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Nov 02, 2012 11:03 am I have to imagine that working at NERV provides a pretty big paycheck. It would be hard making that kind of money and then moving away and re-establishing a life without that level of income.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Nov 02, 2012 11:42 am I would have to imagine that after the first couple of attacks anyone who stayed did so because they work for NERV and know at least a little of what's actually going on. Still wondering how NERV explains that all angel attacks happen there and only there though without spilling the beans on Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Nov 02, 2012 11:44 am Probably something like "They're attracted to the Eva's because 01 beat one of them."

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Nov 02, 2012 11:54 am That or "it's Japan when isn't there a Kaiju attacking."
